mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, with accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive policy rates and elevated inflation creates stagflationary pressure, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 60% of instances when both FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and CPIAUCSL > 300.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms macroeconomic expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% correlates with negative forward returns in > 58% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect labor market tightness and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll gains consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial output and retail spending confirms healthy economic momentum without inventory imbalances, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Divergence between INDPRO and RSAFS trends negates the signal, with forward returns showing no statistical significance in backtests.
